programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami

Drama, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble stumbles into the intrigues of a politically ambitious man when he rescues a leading citizen and his companion from a plane crash

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A tax official asks Perry for advice when he is accused of theft, and later murder, after $153,000 in estate money is stolen from his car

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: CD investigates the death of a friend's son despite his demise being regarded as an accident, while Walker and Trivette track an escaped prisoner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Sharon witness a robbery committed by someone they know, leaving them agonising over whether to turn a blind eye or inform on a mate. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey's house becomes infested with mice, forcing her to shelve plans for a perfume-selling party. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on the home of larger-than-life lottery-winners the Hendersons and Stuart pursues a link with the hit-and-run incident that put Rod Jessop in hospital. Forensics reveal it is the third in a series of fires and the pressure is on to find the culprit before someone gets killed. There's good news for Sam, and June is so relieved to see Rod on the road to recovery she accepts his proposal

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark calls the police when Daisha goes missing, but as they focus inquiries on him, he begins to fear the worst. Faye tells Joseph their relationship is finished, but Elliot tries to intervene. Michael is still discovering how working with Annalese will affect his personal and professional lives, and Linden realises he has become too involved with Callie and her problems

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie's son Louis responds to treatment and his grandad Kenneth has a Christmas surprise for the whole family. After finding out she is a diabetic, Bex drowns her sorrows in wine and cigarettes, unaware of the danger she is putting herself in. Jim whisks Nikki away for a cosy weekend at his remote country house, but the candlelit evening is ruined when Andy turns up

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. Kezia sees a softer side to Smithy as he calmly solves a heated dispute and Lewis dreads living on his own when his gran announces she's going back to Jamaica. Sam tells ex-lovers Stuart and Phil she is to be their new boss

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruth is annoyed with Mark over his gambling, Pat consults a solicitor, and Grant is annoyed Sam is hanging round with Tiffany

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Arthur have a misunderstanding about what to do with money given by Mark. Bianca's world is falling apart over David's continuing friendship with Sam.

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: An elderly Sarah reveals the truth about Bert Ford's death. Meanwhile in 1953, Sarah and Anna start their trial periods in their new homes, and Jack is furious at Carolyn for having kept their daughter a secret. Olivia becomes concerned over James's brutal hospital treatment and Elizabeth makes a shock announcement. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part one of two. A lonely patient develops a crush on Andrew after he helps her overcome an operation that went wrong, but things turn nasty when her feelings aren't reciprocated. Meanwhile, Carol also finds herself in a quandary as it becomes clear that her new male friend has an unorthodox method of pain relief. Rural drama, starring Gary Mavers and Deborah Grant

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A local rascal goes on the straight and narrow to pay for his dog's veterinary bills, and Tristan has designs on Debbie Mount - but her father does not approve. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester falls out with the mayor, after attempting to discipline his daughter, and tries to explain the concept of fair play by referring to the rules of cricket. Meanwhile, William is having problems with their own daughter, Emma. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin ropes the reluctant Yellowcoats into entering the town carnival, and puts Spike in charge of designing their float. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth's wealthy acquaintance Mrs Fortescue, whose sister married a baronet, asks her for a lift into town - but things do not go according to plan. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As Barry tires of golf and looks for a new hobby, Billy's latest idea of an archery course is right on target. Long-running comedy series, starring Mike Grady, Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to investigate why newly planted roses at an upmarket preparatory school keep dying. After witnessing the arcane ritual of the Night of the Stag, they detect tensions between modernising headmaster Quentin Marshall and established members of his staff who are harbouring secrets someone is prepared to kill for. Drama, guest starring Anthony Andrews (Brideshead Revisited), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Supermodel Lula Landry falls to her death from a Mayfair balcony and the police conclude that she committed suicide, but Lula's adoptive brother John Bristow believes she was murdered and that key evidence has been overlooked. He calls upon private investigator Cormoran Strike to examine the death further and the penniless and effectively homeless PI takes the case. Crime thriller based on books written by JK Rowling under the pseudonym Robert Galbraith, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gerry is spooked by the discovery of UCOS's latest victim - a cop, whose body has lain untouched under a basement for 30 years. As accusations of police corruption and underhand dealings begin to surface, Gerry has to face up to some skeletons in his own closet and it's a race against time for the team to help before his murky past catches up with him. While babysitting his grandson, Gerry becomes convinced that only he holds the key to the investigation and goes Awol to uncover the truth. Starring Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A group of environmental terrorists seize control of the Thames Barrier, threatening to flood London unless the Government release a secret document - one with far-reaching implications for the future of the planet. Adam and Ros try to diffuse the situation while a counter-offensive is planned, but end up putting their own lives in danger. Guest starring Cheryl Campbell and Anthony Flanagan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. Kezia sees a softer side to Smithy as he calmly solves a heated dispute and Lewis dreads living on his own when his gran announces she's going back to Jamaica. Sam tells ex-lovers Stuart and Phil she is to be their new boss

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The maverick High Court Judge's daughter is arrested during a demonstration, presenting him with the challenge of getting her off the hook without falling foul of powerful enemies he has made in his single-minded push to the top of the legal profession. Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden and Caroline Langrishe star

However, the biggest surprise comes when Baz and Charlie share an intimate moment under the Northern Lights, and realise they were always meant to be soul mates

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger rescues probation officer Paul Brookes, who has been beaten and locked in the boot of his own car. Further investigations reveal the assailant's wife is a prisoner at Burnwood who claims she has been raped by Brookes. Gina turns up at court to give evidence, only to find the magistrate is an old flame, who promptly wipes the floor with her - before asking her out to dinner. Lewis finds a new home via the nurses' noticeboard at St Hugh's Hospital

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline gets some holiday brochures, Carol and Peggy cross swords in the Vic, Ricky has an announcement to make and David invites Sam out to a night club

programme.desc: Mark and Ruth are thrown off course when Arthur tries his hand at public speaking. Bianca plans for the future while Ricky puts the breaks on. Nigel wonders if he can be a good dad

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Those involved in Bert's death and its subsequent cover-up search for inner peace. Sarah battles her own demons, but is aware that Colin's suffering is greater. Meanwhile, James' treatment continues to cause him suffering and Jack is determined to find answers after Carolyn's revelation. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part two of two. Andrew's career hangs in the balance as the GMC hearing deliberates over charges of misconduct with infatuated former patient Gillian Saville. Drama, starring Gary Mavers

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's plans to celebrate his anniversary in style seem doomed when all his fellow vets want the same night off - and a goat makes matters worse by eating his cheque book. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William learns that getting fired in French sounds just the same as it does in English, but the Fields know they can always go back to England if the worst comes to the worst

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin hatches a hare-brained scheme to stop a hospital being built next to the camp site, ordering staff to make a din from dawn till dusk while the council committee conduct an inspection visit. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's comedy, starring Ruth Madoc, Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans a candlelit supper to impress Emmet with her singing talents, but fate intervenes and the event does not go as expected. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard is dismayed when Marina unwittingly buys him a life-like gorilla which attracts the attention of the local police. Meanwhile, a newspaper article prompts Truly, Clegg and Billy to try to track down Cyril Cooper - a man renowned for his green fingers and womanising ways. Comedy, starring Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The shrewd spinster is sent to convalesce in the tropical paradise of Barbados, where she finds life decidedly dull - until a grisly double murder breaks the monotony and presents her with an unforeseen opportunity to do some serious sleuthing. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Donald Pleasence and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Danny takes Gerry on the run as they search for the proof they need to prove his innocence - despite Gerry remaining tight-lipped about his involvement with dodgy dealings in his old unit. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An undercover operation planned to assassinate a suspected terrorist in Tehran results in disaster. Innocent civilians are allowed to die but the target survives and asks to defect. It then emerges Section D were set up, and the plot was really a means of releasing a lethal virus into Iran - which the team have brought back with them to Britain. Starring Rupert Penry-Jones and Raza Jaffrey

programme.desc: Elizabeth enlists Prudence's help, and on the eve of her engagement party, Sarah confides to her aunt about a shameful secret. Meanwhile, Anna and Gino's relationship moves forward and James' treatment takes a disturbing turn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Having barely had time to settle in at the Beeches, Will finds himself at odds with fellow doctor Sam over a dialysis patient who appears to be pressuring his younger half-brother into donating a kidney. Meanwhile, Alice makes a shocking discovery regarding the secret life of TV repair man Duggie. Medical drama, starring Joseph Millson and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's plans for an evening out are spoiled when a dog goes missing after an operation. Siegfried struggles to keep the budget under control, and Tristan's love life creates more trouble. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A tattered shoebox filled with money arrives for Jeffrey, with instructions from Joe Maplin that it be used to bribe local councillors to not build a hospital next to the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted gets up to his usual tricks, using every money-raising fiddle in the book to fend off bailiffs threatening him with prison for failing to pay maintenance to his ex-wife. Paul Shane and Simon Cadell star in Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Relishing the social prestige it confers, Hyacinth decides that she and husband Richard should have a weekend at a golfing hotel so that she can keep up with the Major and his wife. Roy Clarke's suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom and Smiler arrange an interesting blind date and Truly discovers his ex-wife's new husband is coming to town. Frank Thornton and Stephen Lewis star

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A brother and sister stand accused of murdering their father and stepmother, and pleading for them proves to be the most complex assignment of Kavanagh's entire career. The task is made doubly difficult by the man's insistence on defending himself and a tragedy within his own family - and Julia's decision to leave chambers does not help either. Legal drama, starring John Thaw, Anna Chancellor and Art Malik

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. With emotions running high, it's up to Ted to pull together his new unit and keep the peace in one of UCOS's most sensitive cases. Starring Larry Lamb, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: With Britain on the verge of a plague epidemic, the team must find a vaccine quickly. To that end, Harry orders the interrogation of spies from all nations likely to have it - a task he hands over to a trusted former colleague, Connie James. Each of the detainees is exposed to the virus - a tactic that helps them locate Zaf, and leads directly to secrets about the government's relationship with Russia. Gemma Jones joins the cast

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton puts his team in the picture about the possible closure of Sun Hill when rumours reach the local press. Mickey and Jo investigate the death of a young mother with a history of drug use and discover she has been clean for some time but has fallen prey to a ruthless loan shark. Roger and Dan talk to a pub landlord who has been attacked at a fancy dress stag night

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills takes on the case of a worker killed on a building site, convinced that the managing director of the company should be held accountable for his neglect of health and safety legislation. However, the Lord Chancellor's department once again try to divert the course of justice to avoid embarrassment - and Deed's ex-wife is appointed counsel for the defence. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

In the first edition, Charles Hanson and James Braxton setting out in a 1959 MG to look for items in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick is shocked to come face to face with his ex-care worker Katy Lewis and immediately demands to know why she never told him that Frankie was his daughter - but he is stunned at her response. Lola opens up to Honey about her relationship troubles with Jay

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Sammyjo makes a shock announcement, while there are potential promotions for the juniors and a mishap at Ashleigh's housewarming party

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff get used to two new colleagues at the salon, while Sammyjo surprises herself with a new love interest and has a decision to make about the junior promotions

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Roisin Conaty and Armando Iannucci joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A climber plunges hundreds of feet down a mountain in the Lake District, coming to rest above a precipice. Plus, a driver ploughs into a car blocking the fast lane of a motorway

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid and Bear have a wild night at Shak's party, Daniel and Joe make a big decision about Harper, and Lily has a surprise for Al

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets two couples who have bought stately homes, including one family who have embarked on an ambitious restoration of a historic building in the Devon countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees the profit-making potential in an old wooden bed at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Other recovered items include a chest of drawers and two tired chairs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball find out the latest news about Team Rickshaw from Matt Baker as the fifth day of the challenge draws to a close on the Goodwood Estate. Plus, Kylie Minogue drops in to the studio to lend her support

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Katy convinces Mick to meet Frankie with her, while Honey drops lunch off for Jay, eager to look after him. Kat is determined to convince Phil to do the job with her - and when she overhears him making a phone call about his divorce, she gets an idea. Sharon is keen to get Ian out of her place but he is yet to tell her about falling out with Kathy.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat has Phil exactly where she wants him - but it backfires later when she arrives home to find he has broken in and is waiting for her. Denise tells Jack she is struggling to bond with Raymond, so Jack suggests she find someone from his past to help. She remembers that the youngster's adoptive father was a pastor at a Pentecostal church, so she decides to start there. Shirley encourages Gray to get his kids back home, as Whitney comforts them in the park

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric wakes up from his coma to find the world in the middle of the Covid-19 pandemic - and that the ongoing crisis threatens his chance of surviving his recent brain surgery. Sacha's colleagues are concerned by how he is taking Essie's death, but he turns his back on them in favour of a stranger, while Cameron struggles to prove that he can be a hero

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the first heat, four professional chefs from around the country face three challenges to earn a place in this week's quarter final. After the initial test of basic kitchen skills, they must prepare tortellini stuffed with Mushroom Duxelles and served with a sauce of their choice in just 25 minutes. For the final round, they must recreate Marcus Wareing's recipe for devilled butterflied mackerel with herb-flavoured mayo and a cucumber and fennel salad

programme.title: Is This Coercive Control?
programme.desc: Ellie Flynn brings together 20 people aged 18 to 25 to find if if they recognise the abusive behaviour known as coercive control, which has been a crime since 2015. Over two days, the group watches a specially written drama telling the story of the relationship between Rachel and Alex, and it becomes clear that there is a variety of different perceptions of the events in the story

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Tobias and Big V help a wheelchair user who is currently living alone in Liverpool to find an accessible and less isolated property. They also think they have found the perfect flat share for Ben and his vast collection of theatrical costumes - the only question is whether he will click with his prospective flatmate

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Incident Response Officer Andy is dispatched when a call comes in about a reported stabbing, while Adam and Orani are dispatched to treat a long-term heroin addict whose concerned mother has called the ambulance service for help. Elsewhere, Kieran and Mick are dispatched to their second patient of the night, an 82-year-old lady who has fallen at home and has been waiting for an ambulance for two hours

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chalet bungalow is given a transformation in Beccles, Norfolk, while developers also take on projects in Cwmaman in South Wales. Martin Roberts and Martel Maxwell present

programme.title: The Unknown Warrior 100
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a unique event marking the 100th anniversary of the burial of the Unknown Warrior. An Armistice Day ceremony encompassing the national moment of remembrance at 11.00 will reflect the original 1920 funeral of the Unknown Warrior, including a performance of the hymn Abide with Me, sung by Ruby Turner. Those in attendance include The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Emergency services rush to find a pregnant woman who has collapsed on a remote moor, and dash-cam footage captures the moment a driver falls asleep at the wheel

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the International Antiques and Collectors Fair in Ardingly, West Sussex, where experts Tim Weeks and Charles Hanson guide the reds and blue teams to find bargains that they hope will make a profit at auction. Meanwhile, Charlie meets one of the biggest stars of 1950s radio - ventriloquist doll Archie Andrews

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob helps a young PC gain confidence, and Jimmi uncovers Al's secret. Elsewhere, Shak is hiding something from Ruhma

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Dorset as she catches up with past house-hunters who have found a better quality of rural life near the coast

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades finds items to restore at Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, including a swivel chair and a sideboard

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and James Braxton search for antiques in Suffolk, before making their way to the Kent coast at Chatham and selling their items at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball are joined by Matt Allwright for the weekly Watchdog investigation, and Matt Baker reveals how Team Rickshaw is getting on after another gruelling day of The One Show Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need

programme.title: Life in Lockdown: A Special Film for BBC Children in Need
programme.desc: A powerful, uplifting and touching film which proves how brilliantly resilient children and young people can be in the face of a global crisis. This film shines a light on children and young people experiencing a variety of challenges whilst they also cope with the effects of the pandemic, and reveals how BBC Children in Need funded projects are providing vital support for families during this incredibly difficult time. Narrated by Emma Willis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete. In the infamous Skills Test, two chefs will try their hand at Marcus' test of sweetbreads with a rustic red wine sauce, before the second pair take on Monica's gazed sabayon fruit dessert. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan ask the artists to write under pressure, before rapping about an issue they care for deeply

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: FOS and Ransom travel to Leicester and Bradford to explore the British Asian rap scene, and attend a block party with fast cars and freestyles

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from the Britannia Panopticon Theatre in Glasgow, looking back at some memorable 'musical buys' of the past and along the way dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers. Christina Trevanion also makes an appearance, paid a visit by Tim Weeks the gramophone guru. Plus, Anita Manning catches up with auctioneer Charles Hanson to learn more about singer and entertainer George Formby

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Peterson gets to grips with some old lampshades, while blacksmith Kev Paxton is tasked with transforming some piling metal. Presenter Jay Blades bags himself some windbreaker poles, but will all three projects result in a profit for the items' unsuspecting owners? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: David Cornock presents a round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from Thursday and Friday's Super League fixtures, including Catalans Dragons v St Helens and Castleford Tigers v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Nigel Planer back to Ottery St Mary in Devon, where the actor reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1960

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody ropes George in to help him with a secret mission. Meanwhile, Tracy gets Katie to join the teaching staff. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites 10 home pottery enthusiasts to gather in Stoke-on-Trent, where they take part in a competitive throwing contest to determine which of them should be named Top Potter. Through a series of 18 challenges, taking place over the course of 35 days, the competitors will be judged on their craft skills and creativity by ceramic artist Kate Malone and master potter Keith Brymer Jones. The competition gets under way as the amateurs take part in a four-day assignment, in which they must produce a set of stackable kitchen bowls from lumps of earthenware clay. They also face a Spot Test, in which they must attach handles to 20 mugs within a strict time limit, and a Throw Down, where the judges ask them to make as many egg-cups as they can within 20 minutes

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Documentary following zookeeper Giles Clark as he hand-rears Sumatran tiger cubs Spot and Stripe, which were born at the Australia Zoo in Queensland and are one of the world's most critically endangered species. To help give them the best start and ensure their survival, Giles takes them home to live with his boisterous family and gives them round-the-clock care for the first four months of their lives. The film gives an intimate look into the crucial early development of the cubs as they struggle through their first crucial weeks, while Giles embarks upon the toughest parenting job of his life and tries to save the species from the brink of extinction in the wild. Andrew Lincoln narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Lewis are at Herstmonceux Castle in East Sussex, where a Chinese wall plaque catches James's eye, and Paul Martin explores the history of the building

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the second couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Mark chapman presents coverage of the draw, as the remaining non-league and League One and Two clubs await their fate. Last season Maldon and Tiptree were drawn against Newport County, while fellow eighth-tier side Chichester drew Tranmere Rovers

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as sacred music fans Polyhymnians take on seaside dwellers the Sandy Shores. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of C for capture, E for effortlessness, T for tantalise, and U for utilise

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces, this time focusing on the impact of the pandemic at the Royal Free London, a world leader in the treatment of infectious diseases and one of the biggest NHS trusts in the country. In the first edition, the UK lockdown is over, but hospital staff are counting the cost of having to prioritise Covid-19 above almost everything else as they battle to treat the patients left behind

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Comedy show hosted by Romesh Ranganathan in which 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain.. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: How groundbreaking research into the psychology of crowds could help prevent attacks on police. Plus, a report on a surge in coughing attacks on police officers

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: The return of the programme featuring the stories of people who bought properties they were introduced to when they appeared on Escape to the Country. Sonali Shah visits a couple who bought one of the houses presented to them on a 2019 property hunt in Bedfordshire, then meets a couple who have put down roots together in the Herefordshire countryside after renovating a property from the 1700s

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a dressing table, a vintage suitcase and a petite sofa

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don harvests his pumpkins at Longmeadow, plants bulbs in a pot and has plenty of jobs for everyone to be getting on with over the coming weeks. Frances Tophill travels to Winkworth Arboretum in Surrey to celebrate the season and to explain the science behind the golden and red tones of autumn leaves. The team take inspiration from RHS Harlow Carr and take a look at their winter borders and the plants which will add interest and drama to gardens over the coming months

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes broadcaster Fiona Phillips to the Norfolk Broads to relive a childhood holiday from 1975, including a trip to a theatre workshop

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman collects journalist and broadcaster Eve Pollard in an E-type Vauxhall, and takes her back to the seaside town of Margate, Kent, where she went on holiday in 1955. They read Girl magazine on the sand, before eating salami and visiting the Dreamland fairground, which was the highlight of young Eve's trip. The pair also stroll down the prom, talking about what living and working in post-war Britain was like for her and her European parents

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody and George's secret operation is now a team effort, including Shaun, who thinks he has invented the perfect cover for a trip to Australia without Tracy finding out the truth

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: In Stoke-on-Trent, nine potters have to make decorative hand basins, as well as add a surface design to plain tiles for judges Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones. They are also challenged to throw the tallest cylinder they can while blindfolded. Sara Cox presents

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are nine weeks old and are becoming quite a handful as they explore the Clarks' home in suburban Queensland. Giles feeds them meat, which brings out some very adult tiger behaviour, and encourages their development by allowing them outside for the first time. The zookeeper also visits the rainforest of Sumatra - the home of the tigers in the wild - to see first-hand the poaching and persecution the big cats are subjected to in their natural habitat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A compilation of valuations from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum, Croombe in south Worcestershire, Crowcombe Court in Somerset and Bletchley Park

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Dr Ranj Singh and Meera Syal go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball talks to the lucky couple who managed to avoid elimination at the weekend. Plus, an in-depth look at the weekend's performances and exclusive behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stars of stage and screen John Partridge and Anita Harris team up with antiques experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw for a Road Trip around Lancashire and Yorkshire. John struggles with how best to invest his £400, but decides to go with his instinct when he spies something theatrical, while Christmas comes early for Anita when she finds a ‘surprise' in a cabinet

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The crew of a Weston-super-Mare lifeboat race to the aid of three casualties who have been caught by a rising tide and are exhausted from trying to swim against the current. On the northeast coast of England, volunteers come to the aid of a father and son trapped against a cliff face, while in Swanage, two lifeboats are launched to attend to a grounded yacht and a badly injured sailor

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: Highlights from recent series of the comedy panel show, featuring clips in which Sandi Toksvig, Alan Davies and assorted panellists take a scalpel to the subject of anatomy

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Drama following five recent university graduates who join the world's most preeminent financial institution. They all aim to make their mark on the world by proving their worth and becoming permanent hires at the bank. But for one of them, it all proves to be too much. Starring Myha'la Herrold, Marisa Abela, Harry Lawtey, David Jonsson and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara is persuaded to go on a date, and tries to repair the damage she did to Marie. Meanwhile, Carol is happily back with her awful ex. Adrian Edmondson guest stars in the sitcom, written by and starring Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul visits the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of puffin therapy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Simon Reeve travels through Cornwall as the county emerges from lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian explores the history of cookery shows, taking a look back at memorable moments and mishaps from programmes featuring Fanny Cradock, Delia Smith, Mary Berry, Jamie Oliver and Ainsley Harriott. He also recalls the highlights of MasterChef and The Great British Bake Off, and reveals why innuendo is an essential element of a great cooking show

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: The story of Donald Trump's fourth year in office, when the president, emboldened by his exoneration in the Russia investigation, invited the Taliban to Camp David and suggested buying Greenland from Denmark. It was also the year that Covid-19 spread around the world. In America, the virus killed more than 150,000 people by the middle of the year - yet Trump pushed back against government scientists. And when police officers killed George Floyd, the violent protests that followed forced Trump to an underground bunker. Now, as the election nears, the programme explores whether Trump has lost control of the narrative or whether his political-savvy fighting instincts will propel him to another victory

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets two couples who have bought stately homes, including one family who have embarked on an ambitious restoration of a historic building in the Devon countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees the profit-making potential in an old wooden bed at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Other recovered items include a chest of drawers and two tired chairs

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a three bedroom semi in West Bromwich, a flat in Stoneycroft in Liverpool and a Grade II listed building in Whitehaven in Cumbria

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three players must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes actor Ian Kelsey to Lyme Regis to relive a childhood holiday from 1973, including a boat trip and a game of pub cricket

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With George and Smiler along for the ride, Woody makes his secret trip to Australia. However, there is a shocking surprise in store there for him. Back in Weld, Shay remains the object of Ollie's unwanted affections. Comedy drama, with Rick Donald

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Judges Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones ask the remaining potters to make 10 identical long necked vases using the Raku technique. This Japanese method of decorating and firing dates back to the 16th century and requires the contestants to take their red hot vases straight out of the kiln and plunge them into a bin full of combustible materials. Sara Cox hosts

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are outgrowing Giles Clark's home and developing adult behaviour, so the time comes for them to be reintegrated with the rest of the tigers at Australia Zoo. Their development is far from over, however, as they have their first swimming lesson and meet the adults they are going to share their lives with. But all is not well with Spot, who picks up a life-threatening eye condition

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Previously unseen finds from the show's travels on visits to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral. The antiques experts include Caroline Hawley, Kate Bateman, Thomas Plant, David Harper and Jonathan Pratt with items ranging from Black Forest carvings to a delicate oil painting completed on glass by the artist Bessie Bamber. Presenter Paul Martin also takes a look at the lavish interiors and the beautiful gardens at Powis Castle in mid-Wales

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Dr Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: The Unknown Warrior 100
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents highlights from Westminster Abbey of a unique event marking the 100th anniversary of the burial of the Unknown Warrior. An Armistice Day ceremony encompassing the national moment of remembrance at 11.00 reflected the original 1920 funeral of the Unknown Warrior, including a performance of the hymn Abide with Me, sung by Ruby Turner. Those in attendance included The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: The history of the 1914 Battle of Mons, when 100,000 British troops were forced to retreat into France by German forces equipped with machine guns and heavy artillery. Professor Richard Holmes charts the course of the conflict and follows in their footsteps as they withdrew south from Mons

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Elsewhere, cameras follow great dane Shelby, as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. The shock of the previous night's events reverberates around London. Meanwhile, rumours abound as suspicion spreads through Soho

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the fourth episode, Angela Scanlon, Nigel Harman and Rachel Parris take a light-hearted look back at the year 2003, from Beyonce's hit Crazy in Love, to David Blaine living in a box over the Thames

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces, this time focusing on the impact of the pandemic at the Royal Free London, a world leader in the treatment of infectious diseases and one of the biggest NHS trusts in the country. In the first edition, the UK lockdown is over, but hospital staff are counting the cost of having to prioritise Covid-19 above almost everything else as they battle to treat the patients left behind

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of the beach as the sun goes down, in a seascape painted in gold, purple and orange

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel gets cold feet as the wedding approaches, while Jean tries to remain calm despite her sister-in-law's interference. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould try to uncover the truth behind three mysterious portraits. The paintings in question are that of a child, thought to have been painted by modern artist Willem de Kooning, the image of a lady, considered the work of 18th-century society painter Philip Mercier, and a portrait of an old general, attributed to the 19th-century German master Adolph von Menzel. The investigation forces the duo to embark on a worldwide quest, taking them from Miami, Florida, to Berlin and a small town in Belgium

programme.title: 21st Century Mythologies with Richard Clay
programme.desc: The art historian explores some of the everyday myths of 21st-century popular culture, revealing the hidden meanings in everything from money, Wi-fi and race to the Madonna. Along the way, he meets avant-garde artists including Clet Abraham, Ingrid Burrington, Molly Soda and Rene Matic, and looks at semiotics, the study of signs and symbols that provides an analytical toolkit to helps navigate advertising and its demands for attention

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. At the beginning of 1945, Berlin remains under the spell of the Nazi promise of salvation, an illusion at odds with the city's daily reality of bombing raids, fires to be extinguished and corpses to be buried. Life goes on as the front lines of the war close in each day

programme.title: A Scottish Soldier: A Lost Diary of WWI
programme.desc: Jackie Kay tells the story of black soldier Arthur Roberts, who grew up in Glasgow and joined the King's Own Scottish Borderers in 1917 and fought at Ypres

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at some of Vera Lynn's favourite performances and biggest hits. As well as the many classic songs that helped unify the nation throughout the Second World War, this retrospective features upbeat song-and-dance performances from 1970s series The Vera Lynn Show. Plus, she joins forces with fellow stars like Harry Secombe, Des O'Connor and Bing Crosby

programme.title: Hilary Mantel: Return to Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould try to uncover the truth behind three mysterious portraits. The paintings in question are that of a child, thought to have been painted by modern artist Willem de Kooning, the image of a lady, considered the work of 18th-century society painter Philip Mercier, and a portrait of an old general, attributed to the 19th-century German master Adolph von Menzel. The investigation forces the duo to embark on a worldwide quest, taking them from Miami, Florida, to Berlin and a small town in Belgium

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of the beach as the sun goes down, in a seascape painted in gold, purple and orange

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. At the beginning of 1945, Berlin remains under the spell of the Nazi promise of salvation, an illusion at odds with the city's daily reality of bombing raids, fires to be extinguished and corpses to be buried. Life goes on as the front lines of the war close in each day

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Military historian Richard Holmes recounts the German invasion of France in 1940, when Allied forces attempted to halt the Panzer divisions' relentless advance. Nazi commanders bypassed the fortified Maginot line by invading via Belgium, sweeping aside a makeshift opposition comprising over-age reservists, a French North African cavalry unit and outmoded British tanks. The Allies eventually launched a counter-attack near Arras - but by then it was too late

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a mountain, cabin and meadow, as seen through a window

programme.title: Captain Jack and the Furious Few
programme.desc: One hundred years ago, with U-boats and battleships terrorising Allied shipping, and zeppelins bombing Britain's towns and cities, Ulster-Scots teenager Jack McCleery was one of 12 young men tasked with a deadly mission on board the Royal Navy's 'mystery ship'. In this documentary, actor David Hayman tells the story of the test pilots of HMS Furious, one of the world's first aircraft carriers, whose secret mission would revolutionise warfare

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rarely-seen home movies are shown together for the first time to retell the story of Britain at war from a uniquely vivid and personal perspective. Cine-reel footage is interwoven with testimony from members of the filmmakers' families and those who lived through the war, to give a first-hand account of life during the conflict. From footage shot by a Middlesbrough dentist who witnessed the rise of Nazism on a family holiday to the continent in 1939, to images of the evacuation of Dunkirk secretly recorded by a young naval officer, the films show momentous events through the eyes of the ordinary people caught up in them

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: The second episode looks at the final downfall of the Nazi regime following Hitler's suicide and the disassembling of the Wehrmacht, and examines how life changed for the people of Berlin under occupation by the victorious Soviet army

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Rachel Billington's 1979 play Don't Be Silly!, telling the story of a woman's experience of domestic violence. Starring Susan Fleetwood and Christoper Godwin

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Two men are brought in for questioning in Paris about the killing of Victoria and confess to having paid her to be the surrogate mother of their baby, while several leads take Rolf and Claire back to Poland. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf, Claire and Karol do not get much help from the nuns as they try to find answers in a convent in Poland, while Rolf then discovers a connection to the case that points to a fertility clinic in Paris. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a mountain, cabin and meadow, as seen through a window

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rarely-seen home movies are shown together for the first time to retell the story of Britain at war from a uniquely vivid and personal perspective. Cine-reel footage is interwoven with testimony from members of the filmmakers' families and those who lived through the war, to give a first-hand account of life during the conflict. programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: The second episode looks at the final downfall of the Nazi regime following Hitler's suicide and the disassembling of the Wehrmacht, and examines how life changed for the people of Berlin under occupation by the victorious Soviet army

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: A look at the hidden secrets of the universe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cool blue waterway winding effortlessly from the far away high peaks and evergreens in a mountain landscape

programme.title: New York: America's Busiest City
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Ade Adepitan, Ant Anstead and Dan Snow look at food consumption in the Big Apple. From dusk until dawn at the New Fulton fish market in The Bronx, they uncover the hidden night time operations, hard-nosed negotiations and price fluctuations of this enormous wholesale operation. Ade also looks into local apple production, Anita visits a cattle farm that supplies the region's steakhouses, Ant visits the New NY Bridge, and Dan heads to Freshkills on Staten Island. Once the world's biggest landfill, it's been transformed into 2,200 acres of parkland

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part two of two. Footage shot by an officer on HMS Kent during the Battle for the Mediterranean, and the D-Day landings filmed by a naval lieutenant on his personal camera

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. In the final episode, the British, French and Americans troop wait to enter Berlin, while members of the Jewish community, among whom there are few survivors, regroup

programme.title: Walter's War
programme.desc: Drama based on the life of Walter Tull, a black professional footballer who enlists in the Army during the First World War. While recuperating at home from a bout of trench fever, Walter learns he has been recommended for officer training at Gailes, Ayrshire - but must overcome prejudice, as well as his own demons, before returning to the front. Starring OT Fagbenle, Ewan Stewart, Kwame Kwei-Armah and Dugald Bruce-Lockhart

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse

programme.desc: Ingrid celebrates her 16th birthday and attempts to transform into a fully fanged vampire. However, things do not go according to plan. Comedy drama following Count Dracula and his two children Vlad and Ingrid as they set up home in Britain

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Sid has an app which flags local eco protests. He and Jas sign up to a bike ride organised to draw attention to climate change. When Sid discovers that participants are expected to shave their heads after the ride (to symbolise change can happen fast), he has an identity crisis: Good Sid wants to shave his head to support the cause but Bad Sid does not want to be bald and wants to pull out of the race. Mr Malone plans to leave school early to watch the Men’s Netball World Cup Final at home. He arranges for Martha and Ollie to teach his Year 7 class and ensures Sid will boycott a tutorial by telling him that his netball mascot ThunderBun is made out of non-biodegradable polyester. But Sid decides to chain himself to Mr Malone’s desk instead to avoid having his head shaved. As Mr Malone is desperate to catch the match on telly, he is forced to saw his desk in half to release Sid. programme.desc: As the NHS in Wales deals with one of its biggest ever challenges, many patients are left wondering what it means for them long term. programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.desc: Katy convinces Mick to meet Frankie with her, while Honey drops lunch off for Jay, eager to look after him. Kat is determined to convince Phil to do the job with her - and when she overhears him making a phone call about his divorce, she gets an idea. Sharon is keen to get Ian out of her place but he is yet to tell her about falling out with Kathy.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat has Phil exactly where she wants him - but it backfires later when she arrives home to find he has broken in and is waiting for her. Denise tells Jack she is struggling to bond with Raymond, so Jack suggests she find someone from his past to help. She remembers that the youngster's adoptive father was a pastor at a Pentecostal church, so she decides to start there. Shirley encourages Gray to get his kids back home, as Whitney comforts them in the park

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric wakes up from his coma to find the world in the middle of the Covid-19 pandemic - and that the ongoing crisis threatens his chance of surviving his recent brain surgery. Sacha's colleagues are concerned by how he is taking Essie's death, but he turns his back on them in favour of a stranger, while Cameron struggles to prove that he can be a hero

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the first heat, four professional chefs from around the country face three challenges to earn a place in this week's quarter final. After the initial test of basic kitchen skills, they must prepare tortellini stuffed with Mushroom Duxelles and served with a sauce of their choice in just 25 minutes. For the final round, they must recreate Marcus Wareing's recipe for devilled butterflied mackerel with herb-flavoured mayo and a cucumber and fennel salad

programme.title: Is This Coercive Control?
programme.desc: Ellie Flynn brings together 20 people aged 18 to 25 to find if if they recognise the abusive behaviour known as coercive control, which has been a crime since 2015. Over two days, the group watches a specially written drama telling the story of the relationship between Rachel and Alex, and it becomes clear that there is a variety of different perceptions of the events in the story

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Tobias and Big V help a wheelchair user who is currently living alone in Liverpool to find an accessible and less isolated property. programme.title: Ambulance

programme.title: Life in Lockdown: A Special Film for BBC Children in Need
programme.desc: A powerful, uplifting and touching film which proves how brilliantly resilient children and young people can be in the face of a global crisis. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat has Phil exactly where she wants him - but it backfires later when she arrives home to find he has broken in and is waiting for her. Denise tells Jack she is struggling to bond with Raymond, so Jack suggests she find someone from his past to help. She remembers that the youngster's adoptive father was a pastor at a Pentecostal church, so she decides to start there. Shirley encourages Gray to get his kids back home, as Whitney comforts them in the park

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric wakes up from his coma to find the world in the middle of the Covid-19 pandemic - and that the ongoing crisis threatens his chance of surviving his recent brain surgery. Sacha's colleagues are concerned by how he is taking Essie's death, but he turns his back on them in favour of a stranger, while Cameron struggles to prove that he can be a hero

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the first heat, four professional chefs from around the country face three challenges to earn a place in this week's quarter final. After the initial test of basic kitchen skills, they must prepare tortellini stuffed with Mushroom Duxelles and served with a sauce of their choice in just 25 minutes. For the final round, they must recreate Marcus Wareing's recipe for devilled butterflied mackerel with herb-flavoured mayo and a cucumber and fennel salad

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Ellie's shock confession sends AJ reeling, while it's a race against time for Maggie to deliver Ruby's message to Caitlin. Poppy takes decisive action against her stalker, and Bob ignores Angus's advice to stall his plans to win Kim back

programme.title: Getting Hitched Asian Style
programme.desc: Saffron Events owner Hassan and his team of wedding planners return. Here they arrange several spectacular events for the wedding of 21-year-old law student Amani and 23-year-old lawyer Ehsin

programme.title: Is This Coercive Control?
programme.desc: Ellie Flynn brings together 20 people aged 18 to 25 to find if if they recognise the abusive behaviour known as coercive control, which has been a crime since 2015. Over two days, the group watches a specially written drama telling the story of the relationship between Rachel and Alex, and it becomes clear that there is a variety of different perceptions of the events in the story

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Tobias and Big V help a wheelchair user who is currently living alone in Liverpool to find an accessible and less isolated property. programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Stoke-on-Trent, where Debbie Serpell has her eyes on some Meissen and Stewart Hofgartner shows interest in novelty nutcrackers, and there is more gold for Tim Hogarth

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as June, Theresa, Churchill and Winston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear is exasperated with Paddy, Lydia is shocked at Nate's admission, and Vinny is uneasy with Mandy's idea

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne leans on Steve during their first day in court, Paul is furious that Todd is working at Underworld, and Sean and Michael embark on a new adventure

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott rustles up some warming winter dishes, including beef and red pea stew with thyme spinner dumplings, before fellow chef Jeremy Pang shares his lamb rendang recipe. The host rounds off with something delicious and sweet - his delicious tropical fruit crumble and nutmeg custard

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve admits to Tracy that Leanne made a pass at him, Sarah takes the factory staff out for a meal, and Billy's bishop offers him a promotion

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining the impact of the Princess of Wales' Panorama interview with Martin Bashir in 1995, when she revealed a royal marriage in crisis with infidelities on both sides. Friends of the princess describe how the public image of the couple's marital happiness was a mirage from the start and how a decade of betrayal was to inspire in her an anger and desire for public recognition of her private pain

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. Railwayman Steff shuts down the summit centre at Hafod Eryri as the season ends, while Alwena also calls temporary time on her Halfwacafe. Plus, wild swimmer Viv goes for a bracing winter plunge and Jo the mountain farmer finally sets foot on the summit of Snowdon for the first time.

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tenable
