programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul is hurt and angry, Amy tells a surprised David that someone fancies him, and Belle is upset over the trouble she has caused

programme.title: Summer on the Farm: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, Angellica Bell and Amanda Owen report on the state of Britain's farms during peak harvest, The programme examines the journey a single fruit or vegetable has to take from farm to shop, and looks at how farmers are coping with a reduced workforce, soaring demand and extreme weather. Alan visits one of the country's biggest fruit farms, Amanda helps out with a potato harvest, and Angellica stacks shelves alongside supermarket staff

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary examines how the Princess of Wales' 1995 Panorama interview created scandal and division within the BBC itself, leading to a serious rift in the higher echelons of the corporation. The programme examines suggestions that Diana's involvement was gained through the use of fake documents, and features contributions from a graphic designer at the centre of the allegations

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Great Art
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores Japanese influences on Van Gogh's art, a country he never visited during his life. The programme examines the tremendous impact that exhibitions of Japanese art in Paris in the middle years of the 19th century had on Van Gogh and his peers, including Monet and Degas. Van Gogh's decision to abandon the city for the south of France in his final years was driven in part by a desire to find an environment closer to Japan, and Asian influences can be clearly seen in the exciting new direction his work took during this period

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which takes place on Thursday 14th November against Montenegro

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining the impact of the Princess of Wales' Panorama interview with Martin Bashir in 1995, when she revealed a royal marriage in crisis with infidelities on both sides. Friends of the princess describe how the public image of the couple's marital happiness was a mirage from the start and how a decade of betrayal was to inspire in her an anger and desire for public recognition of her private pain

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. Railwayman Steff shuts down the summit centre at Hafod Eryri as the season ends, while Alwena also calls temporary time on her Halfwacafe. Plus, wild swimmer Viv goes for a bracing winter plunge and Jo the mountain farmer finally sets foot on the summit of Snowdon for the first time.

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary examines how the Princess of Wales' 1995 Panorama interview created scandal and division within the BBC itself, leading to a serious rift in the higher echelons of the corporation. The programme examines suggestions that Diana's involvement was gained through the use of fake documents, and features contributions from a graphic designer at the centre of the allegations

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Great Art
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores Japanese influences on Van Gogh's art, a country he never visited during his life. The programme examines the tremendous impact that exhibitions of Japanese art in Paris in the middle years of the 19th century had on Van Gogh and his peers, including Monet and Degas. Van Gogh's decision to abandon the city for the south of France in his final years was driven in part by a desire to find an environment closer to Japan, and Asian influences can be clearly seen in the exciting new direction his work took during this period

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which takes place on Thursday 14th November against Montenegro

Meanwhile, Brian's drivers' licence expires and Stewie volunteers his services as an instructor

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan discovers a valuable salt deposit beneath the Smiths' back garden, but when he tries to sell the mining rights, he discovers he signed them over to Steve years ago

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger talks Francine into taking advantage of Stan's absence by playing the 'Widow Game', which involves the two of them posing as widows in order to gain sympathy - and free stuff. However, the fun is spoiled when Francine begins to seriously contemplate how she would survive if Stan really were to die. Meanwhile, Steve and his friends stage wrestling matches in the basement, but things get out of hand when an uninvited participant shows up

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: This time, Keith takes presenter Maya Jama for a round of crazy golf and has a playdate with Take That's Howard Donald at Diggerland

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: While looking for a college for Roberta, Cleveland returns to his alma mater during homecoming in a bid to relive the old days. However, he is disappointed when his former fraternity gives him the cold shoulder. Meanwhile, Rallo tries to help Cleveland Jr sleep without his stuffed tiger, Larry

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr volunteers for a church trip after falling for a girl in the choir, but his friend Hunter criticises him for only attending to spend time with her. Meanwhile, Donna's ex-boyfriend returns from Iraq to discover she is now married. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of guest stars Darren Criss (Glee) and Fergie (Black Eyed Peas)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Lyndsey's affair is under threat of exposure when their friend, Stephanie, catches them at a hotel. Lyndsey persuades Alan to ask Stephanie on a date to not raise suspicion. Meanwhile, Walden and Jenny wake up in bed together after a night partying. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan spends time with Lyndsey's boyfriend in the hope of discovering why she likes him so much, while Walden encourages Jenny to take her acting career more seriously. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Dane Baptiste, George Lewis, Emily Lloyd-Saini, Jonah Ray, Adam Rowe, Alex Edelman and Joel Dommett are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona worries that Steve could get hurt by Fraser's friends. Liz is grateful when Fraser gives her a pay rise. Tricia is thrilled when she is allowed to hold her baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha confides in Curly that she hates sex. Ashley instructs Don to keep out of his love life. Liz is stunned when Fraser buys her a car

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is shocked to find Kelly in bed with Biff. Sarah refuses to let Jack collect any of his possessions. Kathy challenges Doug about the woman in the photo

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric sends Sam and Marlon to France to pick up cheap booze. Betty tells Biff to take some time away to help him get over Linda. John reveals his relationship with Steve to Tara

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Debbie Bellamy goes on a late-night drinking spree by the river, but her actions get her into trouble when evidence ties her to the spot where a body is fished out of the water. Rob and Helen try to patch up their relationship, while Peggy and David are appalled to find a hippie encampment in the back yard - until they realise there's money to be made. Rural drama, with Josefina Gabrielle

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean become worried about their lovesick son, and invite the object of his affections to tea

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The first episode of the whimsical rural drama based on HE Bates' novels. Tax collector Cedric Charlton visits the Larkin household intent on getting the head of the family - Pop - to correctly fill out his tax forms. In so doing he falls for attractive daughter Mariette. Starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: In 1935, beleaguered Bournemouth widow Louisa Durrell decides to uproot her family and build a new life on the sun-drenched island of Corfu. However, with money problems and four unruly children to care for, even a new start might not be enough to banish her troubles. Period drama, starring Keeley Hawes, based on Gerald Durrell's trilogy of Corfu memoirs, which includes My Family and Other Animals

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Miller's old Army captain shows up to make him a business proposition, which he turns down without regret. However, when Walker and Belay are called to investigate a vehicle theft, it becomes clear the sergeant's old friend has other matters to take care of in Aidensfield. Meanwhile, Gina is reluctant to let Jack paint her in the nude. Rural Sixties drama, with John Duttine and Jonathan Kerrigan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy and Zoe want to relieve Jan of her duties. Tara teases Steve about his secret life. Marlon and Sam's van is hijacked when they return from France

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sam and Marlon spend the night in casualty. Andy is mad with his dad for causing trouble. Steve asks Tara not to say anything about his past history

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan warns Fiona to keep away from Fraser. Fraser tells Liz he wants her not to dress too modestly. Tony is involved in an accident in his new sports car

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is furious when Tony fails to turn up for work. Liz is stunned when Steve tells her Fraser has got a wife. The news of Joyce's death spreads around the residents

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's cousin visits the Abbott household, and the bemused family have a terrible time getting rid of him. Sid James stars

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert's got a new job as a GP in Yorkshire, but how will he tell Ida that they're moving away?

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Taxman Charley decides to escape from the office and go strawberry-picking with Mariette and the rest of the Larkin family - a move that leaves his career hanging in the balance. Light-hearted drama based on the novels by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry falls dangerously ill, and it falls to Theo and his neighbour Sven to put their rudimentary medical skills to the task of saving his life. The family's money worries continue, but Spiros brings them good news from a surprising source. Gerry adds to his growing menagerie, while Margo is emotionally wounded - but gets little sympathy from Leslie

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Despite Rob's efforts to keep his wedding secret, his sisters enlist the help of the community to organise a surprise reception as the policeman prepares to marry Helen. A man's complaint about noisy neighbours turns sour when his mentally ill wife takes matters into her own hands, and Gina learns Jack's nude painting of her is to go on public display. Drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Doug ropes Eric into dressing up as Father Christmas for the children's party. Jack is put out at not being able to give his children their Christmas presents

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is pleased to go to Robert's school play. Billy tells Andy about all the people who are after him. Kim receives a mystery cassette in the post

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz makes herself at home in Fraser's flat, Gary registers Joyce's death and organises the funeral, while Tony tells Judy how sorry he is about her mum's death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy tells Liz he saw her kissing Fraser. Steve is thrilled when Fiona visits him in prison, and warns her that Alan is putting all of them in danger going after Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: Vivien shocks her children by announcing she plans to sell their childhood home and go travelling with Mark. Each of the three siblings also has personal problems of their own to contend with - Helen's actions at work are beginning to have an impact on her marriage, Jake is shocked to discover that Leila might be moving on, and Natalie's affair starts to threaten her physical safety

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert, Angie and Ida's move north goes from bad to worse when they eventually arrive only to discover the furniture hasn't

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Margo takes great pride in her new job at the surgery, but after a chance encounter with a recluse, she begins to realise where her greatest talent lies. Louisa grows closer to Sven, but as Gerry befriends a convict and Leslie begins spending all his time in the local bars, she begins to suspect she has made a mistake bringing her children to Corfu

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield's policemen struggle to deduce who could be behind a spate of sheep rustling, while Peggy is intrigued by an eco-warrior couple's slurry-fuelled generator. Helen is called in after the body of an elderly woman is found in the river, while the dead woman's son finds it difficult to deal with the consequences of his mother's demise. Drama, starring Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara teases Steve about their kiss. Kathy gets upset when Doug puts his wife first. Vic tries to persuade Viv that the two of them should get closer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel suggests to Kathy that they gatecrash the golf club dinner. Kim confides in Steve about the crank calls. Paddy swears undying love to Mandy and gets down on one knee

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan admits to Fiona that he is jealous of her and Steve. Tony arrives at the last moment to attend Joyce's funeral. Fraser catches Liz searching through his belongings

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-cum-Latterly gather to hold a protest meeting about proposals for a supermarket in the centre of the village, but proceedings are brought to a halt by the discovery of the site adviser's body. Barnaby and Jones discover the victim had a penchant for acting out role-play scenarios with local women - but that is not the only secret the villagers are hiding. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: The children's ongoing distrust starts to put a strain on Vivien and Mark's relationship. Meanwhile, Natalie struggles deal with her situation with Tony, and Jake turns to Stella for advice. Later, Jake unearths a darkly disturbing secret about Mark's past. Starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: Jack is pleased to go to Robert's school play. Billy tells Andy about all the people who are after him. Kim receives a mystery cassette in the post

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz makes herself at home in Fraser's flat, Gary registers Joyce's death and organises the funeral, while Tony tells Judy how sorry he is about her mum's death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy tells Liz he saw her kissing Fraser. Steve is thrilled when Fiona visits him in prison, and warns her that Alan is putting all of them in danger going after Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: Vivien shocks her children by announcing she plans to sell their childhood home and go travelling with Mark. Each of the three siblings also has personal problems of their own to contend with - Helen's actions at work are beginning to have an impact on her marriage, Jake is shocked to discover that Leila might be moving on, and Natalie's affair starts to threaten her physical safety

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

Drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert, Angie and Ida's move north goes from bad to worse when they eventually arrive only to discover the furniture hasn't

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Margo takes great pride in her new job at the surgery, but after a chance encounter with a recluse, she begins to realise where her greatest talent lies. Louisa grows closer to Sven, but as Gerry befriends a convict and Leslie begins spending all his time in the local bars, she begins to suspect she has made a mistake bringing her children to Corfu

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield's policemen struggle to deduce who could be behind a spate of sheep rustling, while Peggy is intrigued by an eco-warrior couple's slurry-fuelled generator. Helen is called in after the body of an elderly woman is found in the river, while the dead woman's son finds it difficult to deal with the consequences of his mother's demise. Drama, starring Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara teases Steve about their kiss. Kathy gets upset when Doug puts his wife first. Vic tries to persuade Viv that the two of them should get closer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel suggests to Kathy that they gatecrash the golf club dinner. Kim confides in Steve about the crank calls. Paddy swears undying love to Mandy and gets down on one knee

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan admits to Fiona that he is jealous of her and Steve. Tony arrives at the last moment to attend Joyce's funeral. Fraser catches Liz searching through his belongings

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-cum-Latterly gather to hold a protest meeting about proposals for a supermarket in the centre of the village, but proceedings are brought to a halt by the discovery of the site adviser's body. Barnaby and Jones discover the victim had a penchant for acting out role-play scenarios with local women - but that is not the only secret the villagers are hiding. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: The children's ongoing distrust starts to put a strain on Vivien and Mark's relationship. Meanwhile, Natalie struggles deal with her situation with Tony, and Jake turns to Stella for advice. Later, Jake unearths a darkly disturbing secret about Mark's past. Starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.desc: Part one of two. Robin and the outlaws leave Sherwood to defend a distant village from a band of phantom horsemen known as the Hounds of Lucifer, who terrorise the countryside on the orders of a powerful coven ruled by Morgwyn of Ravenscar. Guest starring Rula Lenska

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Helen Pritchard finds a man wandering around who is supposed to be dead. This leads Steed to investigate the Happy Meadows funeral parlour, where the dead bodies seem pretty limber. Roy Kinnear, Fulton Mackay and Sally Nesbitt guest star, with Patrick Macnee

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Bearer bonds go missing from gangster Bobby Altman, and suspect Billy Gilpin appears at Arthur's house wanting to get out of town. programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Robin is trapped on a lonely hillside by the Sheriff's men, and sacrifices his life so that Much and Marian can escape. Michael Praed stars, making his last appearance as the legendary hero

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A hijacked security van mysteriously disappears, and the stolen loot leaves no clues for the mismatched duo. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley is tasked with finding out whether a woman is crying wolf with her tales of being terrorised by a masked stalker. Suspicion falls on one of her work colleagues, an inveterate human rights campaigner, who keeps pictures of her in his flat. Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Passenger experience manager Demi deals with some disgruntled passengers, while check-in attendants Carrie-Anne and Selina tackle broken baggage belts. Elsewhere, Border force worker Bob has to decide whether he should let a young Brazilian student into the UK to visit his brother

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: In the immigration department, officers Kat and Bob come across a visitor whose reason for entering the country does not add up. Elsewhere, Demi and Sue, who work in Terminal 2, have to wrestle with a bare chested drunk passenger, and the programme also reveals some of the secrets behind parking planes

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole takes a wild ride through Gran Canaria, and visits the Morgan factory to learn about their iconic three wheelers and what they are like to ride. The presenters also track down a rare 1970s Chopper to restore

Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Angelina Jolie, Giovanni Ribisi and Robert Duvall

programme.desc: A heavy vehicle recovery team has a tough job in store when a 44-ton lorry ploughs across a roundabout and gets stuck 60ft down an embankment, while RAC vehicle technician Mark rushes to rescue a driver who has broken down on the hard shoulder of a windy motorway bridge

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: James Carrington and Dan Machin race to assist colleagues who are pursuing a stolen car that is racing along the M1 at 100 mph and showing no signs of stopping - and to make matters worse it is recklessly weaving across all four lanes of the busy motorway. Meanwhile, the Tactical Support Group heads up a dawn raid on a warehouse, targeting a man with known links to organised crime

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A passenger drops her overnight bag on the tracks and threatens to jump down to retrieve it, while a major power cut at Maidenhead has caused signalling to fail on all the tracks, including the main line into Paddington. At Great Western Railway's Long Rock Depot, the famed Night Riviera sleeper service departs once more after a three-month suspension due to the pandemic, but with radical changes

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: A look at the development of the London railway terminus from its opening in 1852 to the present, as it has been transformed it into one of the best connected places in Britain. In Victorian Britain, King's Cross became a thriving industrial hub, bringing in goods to feed and power London, while 20 industrial buildings that once its beating heart have been given a new lease of life and is now a tech hub that is home to Google, Facebook and Youtube

programme.title: Sex on the Breadline: Adults Only!

programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: The people making a very good living from selling sex. Multi-millionaire Chelsea celebrates a bumper year for her business, while 19-year-old escort Ellie prepares for her birthday party that will last days. However away from the financial success, Chelsea and her cousin - and business partner - Becky discuss the prejudice that Chelsea faces as a sex worker and how it has impacted on her life.

With emotions running high, can she cut a new deal?

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on two residential disputes that escalated from complaints about noise-making to far more serious quarrels. Warren and Sharee detail how their peaceful lives in a quiet Birmingham suburb were ruined when new neighbour Harry moved in. Harry complained of noise the pair knew wasn't coming from their household, an accusation he would level more than once, and with increasing intensity. Finally, when the hostile Harry pulled out a gun and threatened the couple over their garden fence, they were forced to sell up and move out. However, even after relocating, it seemed their nightmare was not over. Also featured is the story of Jill, who was just a teenager when Barry, who lived with his parents next-door, became fixated on her family making a racket. Brushing off the grievance, Jill and her relatives tried to get on with their lives, but one autumn day the feud took a tragic, life-changing turn

She and her children are determined not to let this ruin the festive season, and decide to spend it doing good deeds to make life easier for other families of soldiers who will not be home for the holidays. programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: A special episode airing on Armistice Day 2020, looking back at the vital role the Tower of London played during the First and Second World Wars, and how for First World War centenaries, the Tower transforms into a huge national site of remembrance

programme.title: How Britain Won WW2
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Arthur Williams reveal the hidden stories and events that changed the course of the Second World War. Throughout the series, they take an in-depth look back at some of the turning points during the conflict, and meet the experts and family members who bring alive the unsung heroes and heroines who made victory possible. In the first episode, the pair reveal the hidden stories behind the opening stages of the War, when Britain stood isolated and alone against Hitler's seemingly unstoppable occupation of Europe

programme.title: The Commuter (2018)
programme.desc: Michael MacCauley, an insurance salesman and former police officer, finds himself caught up in a conspiracy during his commute home. After receiving a phone call from a mysterious stranger, Michael is forced to go undercover to save the lives of the other passengers on the train. Thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Vera Farmiga and Patrick Wilson

Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: El Dorado (1966)
programme.desc: An ageing gunfighter is drawn into the conflict between a land-grabbing cattle baron and a ranching family, but gets wounded in the process. A year later, he returns to town to find the situation has not improved - so along with his sidekick and the drunken town sheriff, he fights to defend the family from its enemies. Western, starring John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, James Caan and Ed Asner

programme.title: War of the Wildcats (1943)
programme.desc: An ambitious cowboy engages in a bitter personal feud with a powerful oil baron in a battle for the rights to drill on sacred native American land, and when both men fall for the same woman, the conflict between them gets even uglier. Western adventure, starring John Wayne, Albert Dekker, Martha Scott and Gabby Hayes

programme.title: Thoroughly Modern Millie (1967)
programme.desc: A naive woman leaves her rural roots behind to seek fortune and romance in 1920s New York. She finds work as a secretary to a business tycoon, but he happens to be madly in love with her friend. However, before long she is wooed instead by a smitten salesman. Meanwhile, back at her boarding house, she stumbles across a shocking criminal enterprise involving the landlady. Musical comedy, with Julie Andrews, James Fox and Mary Tyler Moore

programme.title: Mother's Day (2016)
programme.desc: As Mother's Day approaches, an array of seemingly unconnected characters all find themselves reconsidering their relationships with their mothers and what motherhood mean to them. Garry Marshall's ensemble comedy in which various families go through dramatic turning points, starring Julia Roberts, Jennifer Aniston, Kate Hudson, Jason Sudeikis, Jennifer Garner and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Nerve (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager finds herself immersed in an online game of truth or dare, where her every move starts to be manipulated by an anonymous community of watchers. Soon, the challenges she and her fellow players are set grow more dangerous, until lives are genuinely at stake. Thriller, starring Emma Roberts and Dave Franco

programme.title: Southpaw (2015)
programme.desc: A boxing champion's successful life is brought to an abrupt end when tragedy strikes. He turns to a trainer coaching amateur fighters in a run-down gym for help and tries to rebuild his sporting career - while also striving to regain the lost trust of his wife and daughter. Drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Forest Whitaker and Rachel McAdams

programme.title: Anastasia (1956)
programme.desc: In 1920s Paris, exiled Russians claim they have found the daughter of the last Tsar, who was originally believed to have been killed with the rest of her family. She is taken into the care of a General and groomed until she can convince Anastasia's cousin Prince Paul that she is genuinely the tsarina and inherit a fortune. Historical drama, starring an Oscar-winning Ingrid Bergman, and Yul Brynner

programme.title: The Savage (1952)
programme.desc: A white man raised by the Sioux after his family were killed by Crow warriors finds his loyalties tested in later life. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Susan Morrow and Peter Hansen

programme.title: Retreat, Hell! (1952)
programme.desc: US Marines have to battle their way through a frozen mountain pass despite dwindling supplies and continual enemy attacks. Korean War drama, made while the conflict was still raging, starring Frank Lovejoy, Anita Louise, Richard Carlson and Russ Tamblyn

programme.title: Only the Valiant (1951)
programme.desc: A tough cavalry officer selects six unlikely comrades to undertake a vital mission to defend an isolated fort against marauding Apaches. His men's initial hostility soon turns to respect, however, as they realise his wily ways will see them through the conflict. Western adventure based on a novel by Charles Marquis Warren, starring Gregory Peck, Barbara Payton, Ward Bond and Lon Chaney Jr

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: Escape Plan (2013)
programme.desc: An expert takes a contract from the CIA to test a maximum security prison by trying to break out of it, but he soon discovers he has been duped and that his captors intend him to stay inside for ever. He hatches a plan to escape, but to succeed he will need the help of a long-term convict - who wants his own freedom in return for assistance. Thriller, with Sylvester Stallone and Arnold Schwarzenegger

programme.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer (2017)
programme.desc: A respected cardiovascular surgeon secretly befriends a teenage boy whose behaviour turns sinister, forcing him to make an unthinkable sacrifice. Mystery drama starring Colin Farrell, Nicole Kidman, Barry Keoghan, Raffey Cassidy and Sunny Suljic

programme.title: World War Z (2013)
programme.desc: A virus sweeps across the globe, turning everyone infected into mindless, ravening zombies. As entire countries fall into chaos, a United Nations investigator sets off on a journey around the world to discover the cause of the pandemic and hopefully find a cure before human civilisation is brought to an end. Thriller based on the novel by Max Brooks, starring Brad Pitt and Mireille Enos

programme.title: Decision at Sundown (1957)
programme.desc: A gunslinger rides into a backwoods town intent on finding his dead wife's former lover and exacting revenge for her death. The man he blames now has total control of the town and has the sheriff at his beck and call. Undeterred, the drifter offers his enemy an ultimatum - he has until sundown to leave the town or face retribution for his crimes. Western, with Randolph Scott, John Carroll and Andrew Duggan

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly (1966)
programme.desc: A cynical, mercenary cattle drover sets out to deliver a herd to soldiers at a Union base during the American Civil War. During the journey, he is kidnapped by a Confederate colonel, who hopes to persuade him to help steal the animals and take them deep into rebel territory. Western, starring William Holden, Richard Widmark and Janice Rule

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)
programme.desc: A teacher is defeated in his ambition to become the marshal of his town during the early years of the American Civil War, so he turns renegade instead. Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Western, starring John Wayne and Walter Pidgeon

programme.title: Kidnapped (1971)
programme.desc: A naive young man is cheated out of his inheritance by his conniving uncle, who sells him to the captain of a slave ship. On board he forms an alliance with a hardened rebel, and the pair make their escape when the ship is wrecked on the Scottish coast. Period adventure based on Robert Louis Stevenson's novel set during the Jacobite Uprising, with Michael Caine, Lawrence Douglas, Trevor Howard, Jack Hawkins and Donald Pleasence

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin (2011)
programme.desc: A reporter's purchase of a model ship brings him under threat from a ruthless criminal, as it is one of a series of clues to the location of the real vessel, which holds a pirate's lost treasure. He sets off on a globetrotting quest to find it, assisted by a drunken sea captain, two bumbling detectives and his faithful dog. Steven Spielberg's animated adventure based on Herge's comic-strip series, with the voices of Jamie Bell, Andy Serkis and Daniel Craig

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Red Heat (1988)
programme.desc: A Russian detective travels to America to oversee the extradition of a Georgian drug baron responsible for supplying cocaine in Moscow. He is teamed up with a hostile Chicago cop, but when their prisoner kills an officer and escapes, they put aside their mutual mistrust to bring him to justice. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, James Belushi, Peter Boyle, Laurence Fishburne and Ed O'Ross

programme.title: Bacurau (2019)

programme.desc: The duo head off for an amazing adventure in Atlantis. Meanwhile, the Citadel of Ricks undergoes major changes as it prepares for its first democratic presidential election

programme.title: Angel
programme.desc: A passionate but ultimately hollow night spent with Darla brings Angel to his senses,and he seeks a reconciliation with his friends. Spooky drama, starring David Boreanaz

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singletons hoping to pair up over dinner include healthcare assistant Anthony, who wants to put his wild past behind him. Is speech therapist Lawrence 'the one'? Wedding singer Georgina has a blind date with property developer Adam, who prides himself on knowing how to treat a woman, while receptionist Bev sits down with former bank manager Howard, who is strangely familiar

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: At the start of the new term, Carli organises a fashion show that Simon is eager to participate in. Will tries to shake off his embarrassing reputation, while Jay continues to lie about his sexual experiences. Comedy, starring Emily Head, Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon goes to his first gig since seeing Take That at Wembley Arena, hoping to impress an attractive girl in the year below, but Will, Jay and Neil tag along and jeopardise his chances of wooing her. Comedy, starring Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Sheldon start a gossip experiment to see how quickly rumours spread among the gang, while Howard tells his friends he is planning to propose to Bernadette - not realising Raj is wrestling with his own feelings for her. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is infuriated when Priya uses her legal knowledge to find a loophole in the room-mate agreement - but his spirits are lifted by a night on the town with Amy, Penny and Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted takes his new date to the cinema, and is horrified to discover the film is written by Stella's new man, retelling the story of how she left the lecturer at the altar, and portraying him as an idiot

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Lily realises she still has doubts about becoming a parent, but she makes a pact with Marshall to start trying for a baby if they see Barney's doppelganger, as he is the only one of the group who does not have a double. Meanwhile, Robin is offered a dream job that comes at a cost - it's based in Chicago

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets the ladies at the Merched y Wawr - the Welsh version of the Women's Institute - and they show her how to make petals using gelatine

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Poppy are hoping to retire to France's Lot-et-Garonne region, so Ben Hillman sets out to find them the perfect place for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's crunch time for Jonathan and Michael who are facing their investors, but their newest idea - to keep goats - does not go down well. Philip and Angelina host the big anniversary party they've been spending weeks preparing for, but with broken toilets and 60 guests to contend with, how will it go? Plus, single mother Fiona battles to get her dining room ready to show off to the mayor

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Boroughbridge, North Yorkshire, helping teachers Nick and Fiona build a large warehouse-inspired home on the side of a canal

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: It's nightfall at Chester and the zoo's 800 nocturnal inhabitants start to wake up. Aardvark Koos is showing a potentially romantic interest in his sister Himba, but the zoo needs to ensure that their animals are breeding from the right genetic pool. It's also time for mouse-deer Una to leave her parents and meet a mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Lori and Morgan, a couple building a unique modern home on a tight budget in Elsternwick, Victoria

programme.title: Grand Designs Revisited
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Helen Gould and Phil Reddy five years after they built an earth-sheltered home in Cumbria. The presenter finds out if the house has survived without water leaking in and whether it has delivered the eco-friendly life the couple desired

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The return of the programme showcasing engineering wonders, beginning with a team building a 35-room, fully functional hotel out of snow and ice in the village of Jukkasjarvi in northern Sweden

programme.title: Secrets of the Superfactories
programme.desc: The inner workings of green car factory VW Chattanooga, nitrogen-fresh Illy coffee, classic yellow JCB backhoe loaders and robot-made Strandberg guitars

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 84-year-old Neil who has fallen down the stairs at home, and his wife Jenny reflects on the shock of realising that their life may never be the same again. Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries

programme.title: Guy Martin vs the Robot Car
programme.desc: The presenter explores the world of automated self-driving vehicles, attempting to build one of his own in his shed. He takes a trip to Budapest for a ride in a completely autonomous vehicle, which is being tested on public roads for the first time. Finally, he heads to Silverstone, to take on a computer-controlled car in a race

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The return of the programme showcasing engineering wonders, beginning with a team building a 35-room, fully functional hotel out of snow and ice in the village of Jukkasjarvi in northern Sweden

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick's first patient is labradoodle Mitzi, who has been hit by a car - and Noel's biggest concern is that she may have suffered nerve damage, leaving her owners fearing the worst. Options are limited for 10-month-old German pointer Hector, who is struggling to walk - he must undergo spinal surgery or face the prospect of being put to sleep, while a Leonberger with a cancerous leg undergoes an operation to remove the tumour

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Scotland to find out about a mysterious condition threatening salmon stocks, Kate Quilton heads Down Under to learn about kangaroo farming and Matt Tebbutt goes behind the scenes of the annual race to get new season asparagus onto the nation's supermarket shelves

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp learns how to make damson jam, as expert Victoria Cranfield leads her through the task of removing every stone by hand

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Allan have been together for 10 years and with three grown up children between them, are hoping to buy their first home together - somewhere they can go to relax and unwind in Manilva, Spain. With a maximum budget of £135,000 at her disposal, property expert Danni Menzies sets out to find the perfect property for the duo and their dog, Sirloin

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cambridgeshire, where first-time self-builders Mark and Helen Farmer are trying to construct a traditional home appropriate for modern living. Charlie tries to keep them focused on the details, but it's the big stuff that threatens to trip them up. When Mark and Helen break ground, they discover Roman and Anglo-Saxon artefacts on site, a surprise that threatens to bring the project to a standstill

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at No 33 in Hunstanton, Norfolk, where owner Janie Thompson and housekeeper Gemma Layfield hope their quirky design and brand of luxury will win the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to La Tavola Calda in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, where father and son team Mick and Chris Emanuele run a basic B&B above their Italian restaurant

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Lansdowne in the Cotswolds for the third day of the contest, where Janie and Gemma like the rooms, but Mick and Chris are determined to find fault

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Ivybridge Hotel and Spa in Fishguard Bay, Pembrokeshire, where Cheryle and Anthony are unimpressed to be housed in an annexe

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to thrash out their issues. No.33 Hunstanton is up first, as hosts Janie and Gemma quiz Mark and Jan over their privacy problems. Next, father and son duo Mick and Chris Emanuele from La Tavola Calda want to put Cheryle and Anthony's sleep issues to bed. Eventually, matters are resolved and the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Orang-utan sisters Emma and Subis are due to give birth at the same time, but after being in labour for more than 24 hours with no progress, Subis is rushed to the vet for a possible C-section. Meerkats Beagle and Huskie have moved to the zoo as the new breeding pair, but their new housemates, the aardvarks, are making them anxious. In an effort to make them more comfortable in their new home, the team decide to redesign the paddock to give the meerkats their own space

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: As Jonathan and Michael prepare their chateau for the big wedding, they have a sewage problem that threatens to take the whole first floor out of action; with 40 guests due, the timing couldn't be worse. Mandy and Steve's pool problems continue, and, with the first guests of the season about to arrive, they also have to contend with a collapsed wall. It's decision time for Philip and Angelina - can they find their own chateau to buy or is it time to return to Britain?

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets the head of a construction company, who is about to build a home for himself for the first time in New South Wales - and has big plans for the end result

programme.title: Grand Designs: The Street
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows 10 households in Bicester, Oxfordshire, as they embark on a five-year mission to construct their own homes and create a new street as part of Britain's biggest self-build project. The first edition focuses on Terry and Olwen and their neighbour Lynn - all three of retirement age and the most senior builders on the street - as they set out to create their bespoke homes. The designs are bold but the construction process throws up several challenges and soon puts the old friends' relationships to the test

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager with anorexia nervosa is rushed into the paediatrics emergency department with dangerously low blood pressure. Her mother reflects on her daughter's lifelong struggle with her eating disorder, and the pain she feels at being unable to protect her child. A 66-year-old man who fell from a great height onto a concrete surface is brought in to St George's, and doctors fear he has sustained organ damage and internal bleeding. His daughter talks about their close relationship and the sacrifices her father has made over the years

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Old Man Tour
programme.desc: A chance to see a live performance recorded at Blackpool's historic Grand Theatre, on what was the final night of the comedian's sell-out, 158-date UK and Ireland tour. Here, Jon takes to the stage to comment - and generally complain - on topics including parenthood, Brexit and haircuts. The question of why nobody but him can correctly load a dishwasher is also raised. Alas, Jon can offer no solutions to all the problems he identifies, but nevertheless provides plenty of mirth as he fails to reach a viable conclusion to his grouses

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and David Mitchell compete against Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. The Brett Domino Trio join Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager with anorexia nervosa is rushed into the paediatrics emergency department with dangerously low blood pressure. Her mother reflects on her daughter's lifelong struggle with her eating disorder, and the pain she feels at being unable to protect her child. A 66-year-old man who fell from a great height onto a concrete surface is brought in to St George's, and doctors fear he has sustained organ damage and internal bleeding. His daughter talks about their close relationship and the sacrifices her father has made over the years

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Brick Hotel in Newtown, Pennsylvania, where the staff have had enough of owner Verindar, who runs the establishment like an angry tyrant. Can Gordon convince her to value her loyal workers and entice customers back to this landmark hotel?

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A champion running dog is admitted to see if Noel Fitzpatrick and the team can fix its abnormal elbow joint, and a couple have a nerve-racking wait while their Rhodesian ridgeback undergoes a high-risk operation to relieve the pressure on the nerves in its spine. Noel also treats an injured dog belonging to a friend from the gym

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Chile to look into coloured crisps and find out how these rainbow snacks get their colour. Jimmy Doherty travels to Spain to investigate one of the trendiest items on the shelves, avocados, and the rapidly rising demand for these fruits which is leading to alarming reports of a crime wave hitting growers

programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is on the Costa Calida in Spain, with Nadine, who is looking to permanently relocate to Spain with her husband Tyler. Nadine's best friend Jannette is coming on the search to offer a helping hand as shows the friends five properties within the budget of £160,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners. Henry and Simon realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps

Mandy and Steve's pool problems continue, and, with the first guests of the season about to arrive, they also have to contend with a collapsed wall. It's decision time for Philip and Angelina - can they find their own chateau to buy or is it time to return to Britain?

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Tamasin and Andy Jarrett, who hope to build a modern family home in the middle of a Kent conservation area, but they prove reluctant to take on any new ideas

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Swan Inn in Pontfadog, a village in the Ceiriog Valley in Wrexham. Ian and Cathy McNeill take their guests trout fishing, and round the day off with a meal in their own restaurant with entertainment from a traditional tenor. However, the next morning is not so harmonious as the guests jostle for access to the only available shower

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Bournemouth, where owners John and Chris Griffith welcome their guests to the Bed & Breakfast by the Beach. The visit gets off to a bad start when hyper-critical Jill is reduced to tears by the discovery that her room does not have a smoke alarm, and the tension continues to mount at dinner, where the hosts regale their guests with racy tales

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Lansbury in Whitby, North Yorkshire. All the guests are keen to have a good look around their rooms after falling victim to owner Jill Winspear's criticism earlier in the week - but her attempts at a guided tour backfire, and the breakfast service fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group's final destination is Charlie and Angie Hobson's Castle House in Denbigh, north Wales. Some of the guests are unimpressed by a day trip to a local spa - but back at the B&B, the previously critical Jill is full of praise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover what the other competitors thought of their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, having put in what they believed their hosts should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has been declared the best value B&B of the week

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests. With the first guests of the season about to turn up, the pool continues to be a problematic nightmare for Mandy and Steve. Dick's wife Angel shows some clever, cheap, wedding tips - but will Dick and the kids like them?

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Tony and Tania, who want to build a million-dollar house on a $400,000 budget, hoping to strike up moving-saving deals with their architect and builder

programme.title: Grand Designs: The Street
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of two more new builds in Bicester, Oxfordshire, whose homes will become part of Britain's biggest self-build project. For quantity surveyor Jack and primary school teacher Hannah, self-build is the only way of getting on the property ladder - and to make things cheaper, Jack has designed the house and plans to build it too. James is also a quantity surveyor, and he and Shannon dream of a barn-like home where they can bring the good life into suburbia. But the project pushes their relationship to breaking point

programme.title: The Kennedys: Tragedy & Triumph
programme.desc: Series charting the lives of Joseph and Rose Kennedy and their children, uncovering how and why this family so significantly impacted 20th century America. This episode explores the devastating impact of John F Kennedy's assassination on the Kennedy dynasty, and the pressure on his younger brother Bobby to become the family's next political hope

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Niki, 34, is airlifted to St George's after crashing into another rider during a charity bike ride between London and Brighton. While staff try to contact Niki's wife Phill, who is almost 300 miles away at their home in the north east of England, 90-year-old Peter is rushed to A&E with severe blood poisoning

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz with guest captain Claudia Winkleman and Johnny Vegas going up against Jon Richardson and Reginald D Hunter. Sara Pascoe joins resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Nightwatch Nation: Emergency 911
programme.desc: In Yonkers, New York, first responders Alex and Jamal face unexpected challenges when they respond to a call about a man who fell from a fire escape

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Niki, 34, is airlifted to St George's after crashing into another rider during a charity bike ride between London and Brighton. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A boxer dog is brought in with extensive injuries to its pelvis and hind legs from a road accident. Pioneering surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick considers a stem-cell scaffolding procedure - but there are significant risks attached. He and his team also help different animals with broken legs, including hedgehogs, and a kitten that has had an accident at home

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis
